description_long: This is a fixed-blade combat or utility knife with a dark, slightly weathered steel blade and a pronounced clip point. The blade is single-edged and features a fuller running along its length, which is typical for military-style knives. The handle is cylindrical, constructed from stacked leather or synthetic rings, giving it a segmented, grippy appearance, and ends in a rounded metal pommel. A simple, flat crossguard separates the blade from the handle, providing basic hand protection. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible ornamentation, and the materials suggest durability and functionality. The coloration is primarily dark, with the blade appearing blackened or blued and the handle a deep brown.
description: A military-style fixed-blade knife with a dark steel blade, segmented brown handle, and a simple crossguard. The design is utilitarian and robust, with no visible ornamentation.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the blade, the fuller, and the segmented handle with a visible crossguard.
description_view_1: End-on view of the handle, showing its rounded pommel and the narrow blade profile.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with the blade and handle details visible from the other side.
description_view_3: Blade tip view, showing the pointed end and the thickness of the blade, with the handle mostly obscured.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, segmented, stacked leather or synthetic rings, rounded pommel, grippy texture
